pattern: A cell can only be the lone 1 in both its row and column, so precomputing each row's and column's 1-count turns the uniqueness check into a single lookup per cell.
hints: Checking uniqueness by rescanning the full row and column for every 1 you find works but repeats a lot of work across cells that share a row or column.
Precompute, once, how many 1s sit in each row and how many sit in each column.
A 1 at (i, j) is special exactly when its row's total is 1 AND its column's total is 1 — no rescanning needed.

edge_inputs.description: a row with exactly one 1 whose column nonetheless holds two 1s: not special
non-square grid with two independent special positions
one row disqualified by its own row count, another disqualified by a shared column

stress.description: sparse random binary grid up to the size cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, min(size, 100))
    cols = rng.randint(1, min(size, 100))
    mat = [[1 if rng.random() < 0.1 else 0 for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
    return [mat]
stress.note: low 1-density keeps a meaningful mix of special and non-special positions
